xBRIEFInfo.author: agent:cursor
xBRIEFInfo.description: Scope xBRIEF for adding a 'strum' chord playback mode

narratives.Overview: Add a third chord playback mode, 'strum', alongside the existing 'block' and 'arpeggiated' modes, imitating a guitar being strummed. Where block plays all chord notes simultaneously and arpeggiated spreads them across the beat by tempo, strum plays a chord's notes low->high in very quick succession over a short window (much tighter than an arpeggio) to simulate a downstroke, optionally alternating downstroke/upstroke on repeats with slight humanization. This is a pure-timing change in src/lib/chords.ts (the mode type + buildChordPlan note scheduling) plus surfacing the option in the ChordsPanel picklist; no Web Audio/synth changes are required (the engine just plays the scheduled note events).
narratives.Requirements: FR-1: Extend the chord mode type (currently 'block' | 'arpeggiated') to include 'strum' everywhere it is referenced (type, defaults, settings, UI), keeping existing modes and persisted settings valid. FR-2: In the pure buildChordPlan, 'strum' schedules each chord's notes low->high with a short inter-note offset (a strum window much shorter than the arpeggiated spacing, e.g. ~20-40 ms per string) so it reads as a downstroke, not an arpeggio; the whole chord still lands at/around its beat position and progressions still advance by tempo. FR-3: Strum works for both single-chord and progression voices, and interacts sensibly with tempo (higher tempo may tighten the strum) and with the block/arpeggiated hold semantics. FR-4: Optional realism — alternate downstroke (low->high) and upstroke (high->low) on successive chords/repeats and/or apply small deterministic humanization to timing/gain, WITHOUT breaking determinism required by the unit tests (any randomization must be seeded/deterministic or omitted). FR-5: The ChordsPanel mode picklist offers Block / Arpeggiated / Strum for every voice. NFR-1: buildChordPlan timing stays deterministic and unit-tested (strum note ordering + offsets asserted); src/lib per-file coverage gate (85%) still met. NFR-2: No engine/synth changes needed; existing block/arpeggiated behavior is unchanged. NFR-3: typecheck, lint, full test suite, and production build pass.
narratives.Assumptions: A-1: Strum is a downstroke by default (low->high); alternating up/down and humanization are nice-to-haves (FR-4) that must remain deterministic for tests. A-2: A sensible default strum window (per-string offset) is the implementer's call; keep it clearly tighter than arpeggiated spacing. A-3: Existing voices keep their current defaultMode; strum is simply selectable. Existing modes/settings and all shipped voices are unchanged.

items.title: src/lib/chords.ts — add 'strum' to the mode type and implement strum scheduling in buildChordPlan (low->high short-window downstroke)

narrative.Traces: FR-1, FR-2, FR-3, FR-4, NFR-1, NFR-2

items.title: src/components/ChordsPanel.tsx — add 'Strum' to the per-voice mode picklist
Update chords.test.ts (strum ordering/offset + determinism) and ChordsPanel test; verify coverage/build
